rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in annex agree blocks within the appropriate AADL scope: component type for interface assumptions/guarantees, implementation for binding assignments.
Respect AADL scope boundaries: component type contracts govern ports and interfaces, implementation contracts govern subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Use only identifiers and types present in the AADL model; avoid introducing package-qualified or undefined references.
Ensure AGREE annex content aligns with the structural scope of the target component or implementation.
Do not assume or invent numeric bounds unless explicitly stated in the requirement or present in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and must be placed in valid AADL component type or implementation scopes.
Assumptions and guarantees referring to ports must be in component type scope; assignments and assertions for subcomponents must be in implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes or vice versa.
Do not encode AADL properties like Period or Priority as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex structure (covered by KSYN-1)
AADL AS5506C standard references (covered by KSYN-7)
Defensive rules for Boolean comparisons or event ports (covered by KDEF-1, KDEF-5)
